data.title: QuickBooks Payroll named best payroll software for 2025 by Expert Consumers after analysis of leading platforms.
data.description: As per an announcement on July 19, 2025, Expert Consumers has declared QuickBooks Payroll as the top payroll software for this year after evaluating various leading payroll systems. The decision was made after considering factors like automation efficiency and tax handling.

data.title: California sheriffs suspect 74-year-old's disappearance is tied to son's crypto wealth after wrench attacks rise.
data.description: Authorities are looking into the case of a missing man in California, treating it as a potential kidnapping related to cryptocurrency. This comes after a series of "wrench attacks" have been reported. The man's sudden disappearance has raised concerns, with law enforcement focusing on the cryptocurrency angle in their investigation. The increasing number of such attacks has caused alarm in the community, prompting heightened security measures. Details of the investigation remain undisclosed at this time as authorities work to resolve the situation.

data.title: Toast and Five Below showcase fast revenue growth (>20%) near buy points in the stock market.
data.description: Five stocks have shown a revenue growth of 20% or more and are near buy points, with Toast and Five Below leading the pack. This information was highlighted in an article titled "Toast, Five Below Lead 5 Fast-Growers Near Buy Points," without mentioning the specific source.

data.title: Crypto platform CoinDCX hacked, $44 million stolen in sophisticated server breach, confirmed CEO Sumit Gupta.
data.description: The CEO and co-founder of CoinDCX, Sumit Gupta, revealed on Saturday that a "sophisticated server breach" led to a cybersecurity exploit.

data.title: Ripplecoin Mining releases new free AI cloud mining app supporting XRP, BTC, & ETH, in response to GENIUS Act.
data.description: The GENIUS Act, signed by US President Trump, has established a federal stablecoin regulatory framework which prompted Ripplecoin Mining to launch a new AI cloud mining app. This app allows users to engage in free cloud mining with popular crypto assets like XRP, BTC, and ETH, earning stable cryptocurrency income settled daily. The Act is seen as a pivotal moment in US crypto policy, boosting market confidence and providing support for legal value-added channels of crypto assets. Ripplecoin Mining's COO, Alex Chen, emphasized the importance of clear supervision for industry growth. The app's key features include AI-driven intelligent scheduling, easy operation, and daily income settlement, making crypto mining more accessible to a broader audience. New users can register quickly for a free trial worth $15, with daily income automatically settled and available for withdrawal. This move aims to support compliance, stability, and decentralized financial growth in the digital asset industry.

data.title: Lawsuit filed against Addiction Research And Treatment, Inc. for not reimbursing employees claimed expenses. Attorneys involved.
data.description: Addiction Research And Treatment, Inc. is accused of not compensating workers for work-related expenses, such as using personal cell phones for company purposes. The incident reportedly took place in TULARE, Calif., and the case came to light on July 19, 2025. This issue is currently being addressed as an employment law matter in Sacramento. **THIS IS AN ATTORNEY ADVERTISEMENT**.

data.title: FHFA's directive on crypto in mortgages may exclude self-custodied assets, posing counterparty risk for homebuyers.
data.description: A recent directive from the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) regarding the inclusion of cryptocurrencies in mortgage risk assessments may lead to the exclusion of self-custodied assets. This exclusion could heighten counterparty risk for individuals looking to purchase homes. The move is significant as it affects a growing number of individuals who hold digital assets for investment purposes, with the potential exclusion creating uncertainty around the evaluation of their overall financial standing when applying for mortgages. This could result in challenges for homebuyers who rely on cryptocurrencies as part of their assets. As crypto investments continue to rise in popularity, the impact of this directive on mortgage lending and risk evaluations is being closely monitored.

data.title: Ethereum Treasury SharpLink shares drop 20% as part of a $6B plan to increase ETH holdings. Other treasuries also buying ETH.
data.description: Over the last fourteen days, numerous Ethereum treasuries, including the company's, have increased their holdings significantly.

data.title: Large XRP holders shifting tokens to VNBTC cloud mining for passive income. XRP prices up 36% this week.
data.description: In London on July 19, 2025, a recent trend has emerged in the crypto markets involving large XRP holders, commonly known as "whales," transferring significant amounts of their tokens to a cloud mining platform. Internal data from VNBTC reveals a notable increase in XRP funding activity on the platform over the last two weeks. The migration of XRP into VNBTC is not limited to a small group of users, with numerous high-volume wallets making the move, indicating a deliberate shift towards passive yield strategies. This movement aligns with a surge in overall XRP interest, reflected in a 36% price increase this week, with a notable 6.7% rise in the past 24 hours. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: XRP's potential for a $20 price surge is indicated by various charts and indicators, pointing towards a bullish trend ahead.
data.description: Various technical charts and indicators are pointing towards a potential parabolic rally for XRP in the coming weeks.

data.title: Fed's key inflation rate surpassed expectations, reducing chances of July rate cut, boosting S&P 500; Trump considers new chair.
data.description: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure exceeded expectations, reducing the likelihood of a July interest rate reduction, much to the disappointment of President Trump. The S&P 500 saw an increase following the news of the inflation rate surpassing projections.

data.title: FIFA Club World Cup could bring $21.1B in global GDP. $1B prize pool, Chelsea FC earns $153M, US economy gets $9.6B.
data.description: The 2025 FIFA Club World Cup had a record-breaking $1 billion prize pool, with England's Chelsea FC winning and earning an estimated $153 million, the largest single-tournament payout in club football history. The tournament distributed $475 million based on sporting performance and $525 million on participation. European clubs received $12.81-38.19 million, South American clubs got $15.21 million each, and clubs from Asia, Africa, and North America received $9.55 million. Oceania's Auckland City earned $3.58 million. The global sports market is expected to grow to $651 billion by 2028. The Club World Cup contributed $21.1 billion to global GDP, with $9.6 billion in the US alone, drawing 3.7 million attendees to 11 US cities and creating over 105,000 jobs.

data.title: Federal Reserve plans two rate cuts in 2025, despite raised inflation forecasts. S&P 500 uncertain.
data.description: The Federal Reserve has maintained its projection for two rate cuts in 2025 despite revising its inflation forecasts upward. However, the S&P 500 index does not seem convinced about this outlook.

data.title: Elon Musk warned against over-investing in crypto but still acquired some coins out of curiosity. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: In October 2021, tech tycoon Elon Musk reiterated caution towards heavy investment in cryptocurrencies, despite his general support for the digital assets. Musk denied owning any Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) meme coin when asked by a Twitter user, X, during a period of significant growth for the dog-themed cryptocurrency. Following Musk's statement, SHIB went on to achieve its all-time high shortly after. Musk stated his involvement was limited to acquiring some ascii hash strings named "Bitcoin, Ethereum & Doge" out of curiosity. He emphasized the importance of not overcommitting to crypto investments and stressed the true value lies in developing products and services. For the complete article, visit a popular financial news platform.

data.title: Asante Gold Corp starts trading on OTCQX Market with symbol "ASGOF", alongside CSE under "ASE".
data.description: On July 17, 2025, Asante Gold Corporation, based in Vancouver, British Columbia, made an announcement stating that they have met the requirements to be listed on the OTCQX® Best Market. As a result, their common shares, traded under the symbols "ASE" in Canada and "ASGOF" in the OTCQX, are now available for trading on the OTCQX. This move opens up new opportunities for Asante in the international market, providing investors with more avenues to engage with the company. Asante's various listings include CSE: ASE, GSE: ASG, FRANKFURT: 1A9, and OTCQX: ASGOF for different trading platforms.

data.title: US retail sales in June surged by 0.6%, hitting $720.1 billion, with gains in various sectors despite previous declines.
data.description: Retail sales in June exceeded expectations, surging by 0.6% compared to the previous month, rebounding from two consecutive declines. The data from the U.S. Census Bureau revealed that retail and food services sales reached $720.1 billion in June, marking a 0.6% increase from May and a 3.9% growth from the previous year. This growth surpassed economist predictions of a mere 0.1% rise. Various sectors experienced an uptick in spending, with miscellaneous store retailers leading at 1.8% growth, followed by motor vehicles and parts dealers at 1.2%, and building material and garden equipment stores at 0.9%. Food services and grocery stores saw a 0.5% rise, while online retailers experienced a 0.4% increase. Nonetheless, department stores saw a 0.8% decline, and furniture and electronics both fell by 0.1%. Despite concerns over borrowing costs and trade policies, U.S. consumers demonstrated resilience in their spending habits.

data.title: British visitor Jay Birbeck explores smart vehicle section at China International Supply Chain Expo, highlighting global innovation.
data.description: On July 19, 2025 in Beijing, British visitor Jay Ian Birbeck was featured exploring the smart vehicle section at the third China International Supply Chain Expo. The event aims to display the extensive range of global supply chain technology. Watch the video at https://youtu.be/rNrc-2FeY24.

data.title: Federal regulators are proposing to reduce a key capital requirement for big banks after years of planning.
data.description: Federal regulators have recently announced a significant policy proposal aimed at reducing a major capital requirement for the largest banks, a decision that has been several years in the making. This move signifies a potential shift in the regulatory framework governing the financial sector. The proposal is expected to impact numerous financial institutions, particularly those designated as systemically important banks. This change has been long-awaited and is likely to have far-reaching implications for the banking industry.

data.title: Global rush as Trump announces new tariff threats, demanding offers from countries by Aug. 1, irrespective of negotiations.
data.description: Despite several countries believing they were engaging in sincere negotiations, the White House proceeded with its "reciprocal" tariff proposal. Countries have been given a deadline until August 1 to submit their offers.

data.title: Trump criticizes Fed Chair Powell for high interest rates, urges lawmakers to pressure him. GOP members stayed civil.
data.description: President Donald Trump urged legislators to push Fed Chair Jerome Powell for maintaining high interest rates. During the initial of two planned hearings on central bank policy this week, Republican Congress members refrained from bringing weapons and focused on the discussion.
